strategy: Bull Call Spread $17/$22 Aug 2026 — limited-risk recovery play while thesis rebuilds post-earnings selloff

wheelStrategy.vixNote: VIX elevated ~20+; premiums adequate for wheel entry at $16 level. Conditions support CSP if stock pulls back to Bollinger support.
wheelStrategy.bollingerEntry: Lower Bollinger Band estimated ~$14.50–$15.00. Stock trading above lower band at $16.20 — wait for a further pullback before initiating CSP.

csp.exitRule: Close at 50% premium captured (target $0.30–0.35). No roll into earnings.
csp.note: CEO Anthony Noto bought 28,900 shares at $17.28 in March 2026 — $15 strike has strong fundamental support. Earnings hard stop: close by July 24 (5 days before est. July 29 earnings).

coveredCall.exitRule: Close at 50% premium captured. Do not hold through earnings.
coveredCall.note: If assigned on the $15 CSP, sell $17 CC to reduce cost basis to ~$13.70–$13.90.

exitRules.entryTrigger: Enter after confirming $15.50 support holds on a closing basis. Post-earnings IV has settled — current 45% is the entry window.
exitRules.profitExit: Close Contract 1 at 50% gain ($0.65 net debit → close at $0.98+). Let Contract 2 ride to $21 analyst target.
exitRules.timeStop: If stock has not moved above $17.50 by June 15 (50% of DTE elapsed), close the position. Theta accelerates sharply in the final 30 days.
exitRules.lossStop: Close at 40–50% loss on net debit (close at $0.65–0.80 loss on $1.30 net paid). Do not average down.
exitRules.earningsHardStop: Close ALL positions by July 24, 2026 — 5 days before estimated July 29 earnings. No exceptions.

options.flatScenario: Stock stays at $16.20 — spread expires worthless. Loss: $125.
options.atTarget: Stock at $21.00 — spread worth ~$3.45 at expiry (if held). Gain: +$220 (net, $500 max − $125 spread cost)... but we close by July 24.
options.bestFor: Optimal: captures maximum pre-earnings decay runway with lowest premium outlay.

options.flatScenario: Same close deadline; paying $0.15 extra per spread for time you cannot use.
options.atTarget: Same outcome at close — marginal upside doesn't justify +$15/spread extra cost.
options.bestFor: Only if July 29 earnings date is pushed into August — then Sept expiry avoids post-earnings IV risk.

options.flatScenario: Paying $0.35 extra vs Aug for time that must be closed pre-earnings. Pure waste.
options.atTarget: Identical close outcome — $0.35 additional cost with zero benefit.
options.bestFor: Not recommended — closes before expiry regardless. Aug is strictly cheaper.

recommendation.title: Bull Call Spread — $17/$22 Aug 15, 2026
recommendation.type: Bull Call Spread (IV ~45% — short $22 leg saves ~$0.40 vs. single call; spread preferred)
recommendation.entryNote: Post-earnings IV at ~45% after falling from ~65% pre-event. The $22 short leg saves $0.40 vs buying the $17 call outright — meaningful at this premium level. CEO Noto's March open-market buy at $17.28 confirms fundamental support. Enter after stock holds $15.50 on a closing basis. Max gain $375 per spread if stock reaches $22 by close deadline.

companyOverview: SoFi Technologies is a next-generation digital bank and fintech platform. Founded in 2011, it operates across three segments: Lending (personal, student, home loans), Technology Platform (Galileo/Technisys B2B fintech infrastructure), and Financial Services (checking, savings, investing, crypto). Q1 2026 was a record quarter: $1.09B adjusted net revenue (+41% YoY), $166.7M GAAP net income, and 22.2M total products (+39% YoY). Despite record results, the stock sold off on Technology Platform slowdown concerns and cautious macro guidance — creating a potential opportunity at $16 vs. the $17.28 CEO open-market buy in March.

insiderFlow.summary: CEO Noto made a significant open-market purchase in March 2026 at prices near current levels — a strong conviction signal.

transactions.note: Open-market purchase — not a grant or ESPP. Filed Form 4. Executed at $17.255–$17.340.
Quarterly pay deferral — routine comp, not a discretionary buy.

insiderFlow.signal: BULLISH — CEO made a discretionary open-market purchase of $499K at $17.28 on March 17, just 8% above current price of $16.20. Strong alignment signal.

catalysts: CEO Noto bought 28,900 shares open-market at $17.28 in March 2026 — skin in the game at current levels
Financial Services segment turning highly profitable: $429M revenue (+41% YoY), up from a loss-maker in 2022
SoFi Crypto launch — first nationally chartered bank to offer crypto trading; early-mover advantage
Q2 2026 guidance: ~30% adj. revenue growth to ~$1.115B with $0.10–0.11 adj. EPS
Big Business Banking launch (Q1 2026) opens new B2B revenue vertical
Post-earnings selloff may be overdone — $1.09B revenue beat ignored by market; creates asymmetric entry
risks: Technology Platform segment slowdown — primary reason for post-earnings stock decline despite record Q1
Morgan Stanley Underweight with $16 target; Deutsche Bank cut to $18 Hold — near-term analyst headwind
Net interest margin (5.94%) exposed to rate cuts — any Fed pivot compresses NII faster than origination growth offsets
Competition from traditional banks (Chase, Wells Fargo) and fintechs (Robinhood, Chime) intensifying
Stock down >50% from 52-week high of $32.73 despite strong fundamentals — momentum traders exiting
dilution: SoFi has ~1.1B shares outstanding. SBC (stock-based compensation) remains elevated but has moderated as the company scales. GAAP profitability reduces the need for equity issuance. Ongoing options/RSU grants are a dilution factor but well-understood by the market.
